metadata.title: CATE Validation: Qini + Calibration Beat Single-Model AUC

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CATE Validation: Qini + Calibration Beat Single-Model AUC

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

props.command: CATE Validation: Qini + Calibration Beat Single-Model AUC
props.segment: CATE Validation: Qini + Calibration Beat Single-Model AUC

beats.role_note: the problem, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: You built a CATE model. How do you know it is measuring causal effects, not correlations? Qini coefficient and calibration are the answer.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'CATE validation — Qini not AUC'; AUC measures correlation, Qini measures uplift

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude. Train a good T-learner and a deliberately bad model on the same RCT data. Evaluate both using Qini coefficient and decile calibration. Print a validation scorecard.

props.command: claude "Write Python CATE validation. Synthetic RCT n=4000, true CATE varies by X1 (large for X1>0, near-zero for X1<0). Build: (1) good T-learner (separate RF per treatment arm); (2) bad model (single RF predicting Y). Evaluate both: Qini coefficient, AUUC, decile calibration (mean predicted CATE vs observed Y|T=1 - Y|T=0). Print scorecard."
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CAUSAL MACHINE LEARNING — CATE VALIDATION

beats.narration_text: Read it. The Qini coefficient is the causal analog of AUC — it measures how much incremental outcome you capture by targeting in order of predicted CATE versus random. Calibration checks whether the CATE estimate is on the right scale.

props.code:
# cate_validation.py — Claude Code output

def qini_coefficient(cate_pred, Y, T):
    # Sort by predicted CATE descending
    order = np.argsort(cate_pred)[::-1]
    # Cumulative incremental outcomes vs random
    cum_treat = np.cumsum(Y[order] * T[order]) / T.sum()
    cum_ctrl = np.cumsum(Y[order] * (1-T[order])) / (1-T).sum()
    cum_lift = cum_treat - cum_ctrl
    random_lift = np.linspace(0, cum_lift[-1], len(order))
    return np.trapz(cum_lift - random_lift) / len(order)

# Decile calibration
for d in range(10):
    idx = decile_mask(cate_pred, d)
    obs_effect = Y[idx&(T==1)].mean() - Y[idx&(T==0)].mean()
props.title: cate_validation.py

beats.narration_text: Left panel: Qini curves. T-learner Qini equals 0.31 — strong positive area. Bad model Qini equals 0.04 — barely above random. Right panel: calibration. T-learner predicted CATE tracks observed effect decile by decile. Bad model overclaims the top two deciles by 3x.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: Qini curves left + calibration line chart right

beats.narration_text: Now ask Claude to add an AUUC confidence interval via bootstrap — 200 bootstrap samples of the Qini calculation. This turns a point estimate into a statement you can defend in a production review.

props.command:
claude "update cate_validation.py:
  -> add bootstrap CI for Qini coefficient (200 samples)
  -> compute 95% CI for T-learner and bad model
  -> show whether CIs overlap (statistical significance test)"
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CAUSAL MACHINE LEARNING — CATE VALIDATION

beats.narration_text: T-learner Qini 95% CI: [0.24, 0.38]. Bad model Qini 95% CI: [0.01, 0.07]. The intervals do not overlap. The difference is statistically significant and practically large — the good model captures 7x more incremental outcome in the top decile.
beats.visual_intent: slate: Qini bootstrap CI bars; T-learner vs bad model with error bars

beats.narration_text: Use Qini, not AUC. A model that predicts purchase well may rank buyers ahead of responders — and that is exactly what you do not want. Qini directly measures whether you are identifying responders versus non-responders.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: Qini vs AUC distinction; T-learner calibration as gold standard

beats.narration_text: Your move: compute the Qini coefficient on your current campaign targeting model. If it is below 0.1, you are targeting buyers, not responders. The validation scorecard — Qini plus calibration — is the minimum bar before any CATE model goes to production.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: run Qini + calibration, check threshold, validate before production

beats.narration_text: Humanitarians AI. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Humanitarians AI, nikbearbrown dot com.
